pi_items.source: Princeton Planetary Systems Database (https://www.princeton.edu/~willman/planetary_systems/Sol/Mars/) and NASA JPL search results
Princeton Planetary Systems Database and NASA JPL sources
Orbital mechanics calculation from verified a and e values. c = a*e is the focal distance
Task description explicitly states 'Model as a circle with radius 1 AU'. This is a model simplification of Earth's actual orbit (which has eccentricity 0.0167)
Orbital mechanics calculation: Perihelion = a(1-e). Verified against Princeton data: 1.3814 AU
Orbital mechanics calculation: Aphelion = a(1+e). Verified against Princeton data: 1.666 AU
Task requirement explicitly stated in task.json description and enforced by export_result.sh

privileged_info_summary: Mars orbital parameters are based on verified NASA/JPL data. The semi-major axis (a=1.524 AU) and eccentricity (e=0.0934) match official sources. Derived orbital characteristics are correct: focal distance c = 0.1423 AU, perihelion distance = 1.3816 AU, aphelion distance = 1.6663 AU. Earth is modeled as a perfect circle with radius 1 AU per task requirements. The file must be saved as 'mars_orbit.ggb' in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/.
